title: NOC 31120 – Pharmacists | GTR Immigration
description: Pharmacists are critical frontline healthcare professionals across Canada. With expanded prescribing authority, aging demographics, and increasing chronic disease management, pharmacists remain in steady demand nationwide. Classified under NOC 31120 (TEER 1), this occupation aligns with:
duties: Counsel patients on drug usage and interactions
Maintain medication records and narcotics registry
Ensure proper storage of vaccines and pharmaceuticals
Adapt dosage, formulation, or administration route (where permitted)
Participate in drug research and development
Ensure product quality control and regulatory compliance
Promote pharmaceutical products to healthcare professionals
employment_requirements: Certification and licensing by the provincial regulatory authority
Licensure is mandatory in all provinces and territories before independent practice.
job_outlook: While competition exists in urban centers, opportunities remain strong in:

pathways: Stable public and private sector employment
Expanded clinical authority in many provinces
Access to Canada’s public healthcare system
additional_information: Community retail pharmaciesHospital pharmaciesPharmaceutical manufacturing companiesResearch institutionsGovernment health departmentsLong-term care facilities
Typical annual earnings: $54,200–$144,300 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and ownership status.
Expanded scope of practice in many provinces—including prescription renewals and minor ailment prescribing—has increased demand.
